intent: Make the API rate limiter configurable through environment variables while preserving the existing middleware ordering and default behavior.
constraints: Keep authMiddleware imported and applied exactly as it is today.
Default to the current window and max values when env vars are missing or invalid.
Limit the implementation to bootstrap-level configuration and small parsing helpers if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: The rate limiter reads its window and max values from environment variables.
When env vars are absent or invalid, the service still uses 60000 ms and 100 requests.
Auth middleware remains in place and protected routes still require authorization.
antiPatterns: Changing or removing auth middleware while touching server bootstrap.
Broad refactors to unrelated routing or middleware registration.
Making startup fail on missing env vars when safe defaults already exist.
verificationMethod: Start the service with custom rate limit env vars and confirm the configured values are used.
Start the service without env vars and confirm the current defaults are preserved.
Exercise an authenticated API route and confirm auth enforcement still works.
scopeBounds: Allowed: src/server.ts and a small env parsing helper if necessary.
Out of scope: auth behavior changes, route changes, database work, or webhook changes.
